item.mcpirates.furled_bounty.no_target: Your sources find no untouched pirate lair. Sail far into uncharted waters and try again.
item.mcpirates.bounty_hunter_guide.name: Guide for the Bounty Hunters
item.mcpirates.bounty_hunter_guide.landi: Welcome, bounty hunter. The sheriff has entrusted you with this guide. Read on to learn how to track and dismantle the pillager airship operations.$(br2)$(playername), let's get to work.
The bounty system from the ground up: who hands out the work, how the loop runs, and what the final reward looks like.

Every village holds a $(item)Sheriff$() — a villager stationed at a $(item)bounty board$(). Right-click them to open the board they keep. Don't expect them to trade emeralds for trinkets: sheriffs only deal in furled bounty maps, captain's seals, and a small purse of emeralds for finished work.
A sheriff retires after handing out five complete cycles. If the board looks $(red)RETIRED$(), find another village — there'll be another sheriff somewhere along the road.

The sheriff's board has three rows of five slots. Each cycle works the same way: take a $(item)Furled Bounty$() from the top row, hunt the airship it points at, return with the dead captain's seal, and collect your reward.

Right-click a furled scroll to unfurl it — you'll get a $(item)bounty map$() pointing at the nearest pillager outpost. The airship lifts off when you get close enough. Kill the captain to drop their $(item)Captain's Seal$().
Hand the seal in to the sheriff by placing it in the highlighted slot of the seals row. The next map and reward unlock immediately.
Repeat. The sheriff will line up the next four cycles for you, each pointing at a different outpost. Don't skip turning in seals — without a seal, the next slot stays locked.

The fifth and final map of a sheriff's cycle is special. It points not at a pillager outpost but at a $(red)pirate Galleon$() — a much larger ship with a tougher crew. Defeating one is the climax of the bounty hunter's path. Once you take that last map, the sheriff's work is done and the board reads $(red)RETIRED$().
